title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. circinatus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. circinatus, also known as the hooked water buttercup or water crowfoot, is a plant species within the buttercup family that grows in aquatic environments, typically in slow-moving streams and rivers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. circinatus include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, but caution is advised due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
